because.text: Accepted in full; gate CLOSED, architecture DECIDED: F1-strict. Kimi's from-first-principles arithmetic reproduced exactly against the oracle: (w1*w2)*w3 = {0,16384,0} vs w1*(w2*w3) = {0,32768,-128} - grouping alone doubles amplitude - and the same-phase entropy split -576 vs -512. The F1.5-vs-strict split adjudicated 2:1 (Gemini's F1 had no profile registry) with Kimi's governance-dynamics argument as the deciding substance: protocol-named profiles guarantee a shipped default, network effects canonize it, F3 re-enters through the social layer immune to non-normative labels - aggregation stays client-local, unnameable, undiscoverable. Adopted: ViewID redesign (jurisdiction root in the hash, per-node, policy-as-hash not string id, assertion_set_root Merkle commitment - fixes decidability, replay-of-the-name, privacy, canonicalization in one move); replay resistance criterion (Warrant s9 shared blob stores make cross-jurisdiction assertion replay live TODAY - blobs must embed their jurisdiction root); conflict-set client rule (ties only, clients MUST NOT merge, automated systems treat conflicted as unannotated); the Book II federation paragraph (waves are per-jurisdiction per-policy derived coordinates, interfere() structural-only, divergence permanent by design, s6 pins = null-jurisdiction defaults) as drafting base; criteria 7-10. Next milestone: v0.6 protocol draft from rev 2 + implementation gate.

subject.note: Kimi ADR-006 gate review 3: F1-strict - GATE CLOSED 3/3 on selection-only, strictness adjudicated 2:1; ViewID redesign, replay resistance, Book II federation paragraph, criteria 7-10
